patches.find: Silent-ignore is PIPELINE-CANONICAL. patches.find: suppressed to ≤30% of the ensemble-median in-band signal (consistent with Kim & Chang (2018)'s published attenuation envelope for latitudes outside the primary response zone)
patches.replace: suppressed to ≤30% of the ensemble-median in-band signal — a conservative upper bound adopted in the absence of a paper-tabulated attenuation factor for >50° CGM stations. Kim & Chang (2018) documents a systematic Z-component decrease outside the 30°–50° CGM band across their 39-eclipse ensemble but does not itself tabulate the magnitude of the drop-off for the >50° stations — so we fix the upper bound at 30% of the in-band median as a first-order, citation-honest pre-registration, with the explicit acknowledgement that if the 39-eclipse binned amplitudes become accessible we will replace this bound with a paper-derived amplitude and re-pre-register.
patches.rationale: H1: Reframe 30% bound as citation-honest conservative upper bound. Kim & Chang does not tabulate attenuation factor for >50° CGM stations. Preempts 'number pulled from a paper that does not contain it' defender attack. EXP-243 / ISS-1496.

patches.find: any nonlinear-coverage effect will drift both residuals in parallel, leaving the geographic-vs-magnetic-latitude fingerprint intact
patches.replace: any nonlinear-coverage effect will introduce an approximately common multiplicative error on both residuals at first order, so the geographic-vs-magnetic-latitude fingerprint remains the dominant discriminator even under moderate nonlinear-coverage corrections
patches.rationale: H2: Soften 'drift both residuals in parallel' — parallel drift is too strong given mag-lat structure of E-region conductivity. EXP-243 / ISS-1496.

patches.find: reproduces all nine published FSF values (0.642–2.075) to better than 0.1% at seven stations and within 1.8% at the worst, with Pearson r = 0.9999
patches.replace: reproduces all nine published FSF values (0.642–2.075) to within 0.6% at seven of nine stations and within 2.78% at the worst, with Pearson r = 0.9999
patches.rationale: H3: Propagate precision correction to detail_evidence (dvt already patched in prior run). Actual figures: 7/9 at <0.5%, worst ~2.16%. EXP-243 / ISS-1496.

patches.find: any smooth monotonic H(r) combined with the dome's r·tan(θ) = H(r) convention produces a latitude-dependent ratio of the same form
patches.replace: a broad family of smooth monotonic H(r) functions fitted to the same sun/moon altitude calibration targets, combined with the dome's r·tan(θ) = H(r) convention, produces a latitude-dependent ratio of effectively the same form over the relevant r range
patches.rationale: H4: Soften unsupported universal 'any smooth monotonic H(r)' to bounded family claim. EXP-243 / ISS-1496.
